manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: mango, topaz, igloo, onion, cucumber, uncle, asparagus, mace, lamp.
Your opponent's team words are: plum, hotel, armoire, herbs, house, ankle, mother, gem.
Distractor words are: axe, apricot, desk, face, grape, cumin, son.
The kill words are: sage.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must be single-word clue, not any of the words or contain parts. Must list target words from our list that the clue relates to.

Our team's words: mango, topaz, igloo, onion, cucumber, uncle, asparagus, mace (spice?), lamp.

We can try clue "vegetable" but includes onion, cucumber, asparagus, maybe also mango (fruit), topaz? not. Can't be "veg" maybe? But can't include parts of words? "veg" is part of "vegetable". Could be okay. But "vegetable" relates to onion, cucumber, asparagus. Also maybe "mace" is a spice not veg. "lamp" no. "uncle" no. "igloo" no. So maybe clue "cold"? Could relate igloo, maybe topaz (cold gem). Not strong.

Goal to maximize number without risking opponent words. Need to avoid linking to opponent's words: plum, hotel, armoire, herbs, house, ankle, mother, gem. Also distractors: axe, apricot, desk, face, grape, cumin, son.

Your clue must not be one of these words nor contain parts of them. E.g., "fruit" would link to mango, plum (opponent) and apricot (distractor). Also "fruit" includes "fruit" not a part of any listed word? That's fine, but would risk opponent. Avoid that.

"Spice" would relate to mace (our) and maybe cumin (distractor) and herbs (opponent). Not good.

"Light" could relate lamp (our), topaz (gem - maybe opponent gem? Actually gem is opponent, topaz is ours, but "light" could also relate "lamp" and maybe "igloo"? Not.

"Family" could relate uncle (our) mother (opponent) son (distractor). Not good.

"Cool" maybe igloo, topaz? But also "cool" could relate "ice" etc.

Maybe clue "green" relates cucumber, asparagus, maybe mango (green mango), also could relate "herbs" (opponent). Might risk.

Clue "candle" relates lamp, maybe onion (no). Not.

Clue "arctic" relates igloo, maybe topaz (cold gemstone) maybe not.

Better to give clue that only hits two words like "vegetable" for onion,cucumber,asparagus. Are any opponent words also vegetables? "herbs" is not in list but might be considered. But "vegetable" not directly herb. Could be safe.

We should ensure clue is not part of any word: "vegetable" not part of mango, topaz, igloo, onion, cucumber, uncle, asparagus, mace, lamp. Not part of opponent or distractors. Good.

Thus:

CLUE: vegetable
TARGETS: onion,cucumber,asparagus

That's acceptable
